title: Layer Drawer Simplification, Snapshot Meaning Clarification, and Report Context Polish

layer_drawer_simplification.summary: Reworked the expanded layer drawer into a compact map-control panel instead of a documentation surface.
changes: Added compact Map Layers header copy with active layer count, reset layers, and collapse drawer controls.
Grouped layers under Base / Parcel, Development Activity, Flood / Constraints, Schools, Transportation, Utility / Infrastructure, Planning Context, and Internal Research / Governance.
Kept Base / Parcel open by default and collapsed other groups by default.
Converted live layer controls to compact rows with layer name, Active/Hidden status, source/status chips, More Info, Configure, Legend, and toggle controls.
Kept live API layers toggleable even when their registry visibility flag is false because their render state is controlled by runtime hooks.
Moved Infrastructure Readiness out of equal-weight layer treatment and into a Methodology/proxy-only row.

more_info_behavior.summary: Long source notes and caveats now live behind More Info instead of occupying the main layer list.
examples_added: Development Hotspots: observed permit/development activity only; not a prediction.
FEMA Flood Zones: FEMA NFHL remains authoritative.
School Utilization Seed: presentation-derived values require official verification.
Utility / Infrastructure: proxy/context only; does not confirm capacity.
Internal Model Research: aggregate governance only; no parcel-level prediction is shown.

legend_and_configure_behavior.summary: Legends and filters are collapsed by default and opened intentionally from each compact row.
legend_and_configure_behavior.off_state_helper: Turn layer on to configure display.

placeholder_layer_handling.summary: Infrastructure readiness is no longer presented like a confirmed live layer.
placeholder_layer_handling.wording: Infrastructure readiness is tracked in Methodology and will become stronger after official utility capacity and planned infrastructure data are available.

snapshot_meaning_clarification.summary: Updated snapshot language so users understand that a Planning Snapshot captures map and intelligence context for reporting, not hidden model output.
snapshot_meaning_clarification.safety_boundary: Snapshots do not store parcel-level prediction probabilities, ranking classes, or hidden model scores.

snapshot_library_behavior.summary: Improved the Planning Snapshot Library as a multi-snapshot localStorage report workspace.
changes: Library cards show map thumbnail or clean unavailable placeholder.
Library cards show timestamp, parcel/map context, active layers count, and Intelligence Brief title.
Open action label is simplified to Open.
Delete now asks for confirmation before removing a local snapshot.

map_screenshot_and_report_behavior.summary: Saved snapshots continue to include map screenshot data URLs when ArcGIS SceneView capture succeeds; report view includes a clean map-unavailable placeholder if capture fails.
map_screenshot_and_report_behavior.browser_result: A new Planning Snapshot was saved during smoke testing and showed a map thumbnail in the snapshot library.

executive_summary_context.summary: Executive Summary now includes a dedicated Intelligence Brief section before key findings and metric explanations.

intelligence_panel_preservation.summary: Adaptive Intelligence Brief and Selected Parcel Intelligence behavior was retained. Snapshot save copy was clarified without changing the panel's core behavior.

browser_check.result: No parcel-level prediction probabilities, ranking classes, or hidden model-score field names appeared in the tested Overview, Planning Snapshot, Executive Summary, or Methodology screens.

remaining_known_issues: Map screenshot capture still depends on the ArcGIS SceneView screenshot helper being available; when it is unavailable, CFS saves the snapshot and shows a clean map-unavailable placeholder.
Snapshot persistence remains frontend/localStorage only by design for this phase.
